mapping.a_element: classical string winding around a compact dimension undergoing contraction and re-expansion in Milne space
mapping.b_element: membrane worldvolume with compact spatial topology Σ_h coupled to critical Lifshitz matter and gravity

mapping.a_element: non-singular propagation of excited string states through a spacetime singularity
mapping.b_element: reproduction of bosonic string partition function via quantum critical membrane ground-state wavefunction

mapping.a_element: compactified Milne space as the background geometry for string dynamics
mapping.b_element: D=2 worldvolume gravity at a Lifshitz point (z=2) as the background for membrane dynamics

brief_justification: Paper A analyzes classical string dynamics in a time-dependent orbifold background, focusing on non-singular propagation through a geometric singularity. Paper B constructs a quantum theory of membranes at criticality to reproduce string partition functions, involving Lifshitz scalars and nonrelativistic gravity. While both involve strings/membranes in compactified settings, their core mechanisms—classical geometric propagation vs. quantum criticality and Bose-Einstein condensation—are fundamentally distinct.
